title: rhododendron indicum var. obtusum
intro: Rhododendron indicum var. obtusum, also known as the fragrant azalea, is a deciduous shrub native to the Himalayan region with pink or white fragrant flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. The health benefits of Rhododendron indicum var. obtusum include its ability to act as an expectorant, relieving coughs and congestion, and its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Rhododendron indicum var. obtusum is typically prepared for medicinal use through herbal tea infusions or extracts, and its bark is also used in some traditional remedies.
